page: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with a single thread per core, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU is based on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 model, equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks x86-specific extensions such as AVX or AMX.

The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This memory subsystem demonstrates strong performance, as evidenced by a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 774.63 GB/sec. The instance does not include local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates balanced processing capabilities, with average tier performance in stress-ng single-core (1533.19) and multi-core (12242.92) tests. However, the Passmark CPU score falls into the bottom 25%, indicating weaker raw computational performance compared to some x86 alternatives. Large block memory write bandwidth is measured at 68.94 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the instance handles Redis SET operations at 13.53 million operations per second, but exhibits poor performance in LLM text generation for small models (40.6 tokens/sec) and prompt processing for medium models (14.16 tokens/sec).

Given its high memory density and strong memory bandwidth relative to its 8-vCPU compute footprint, the r7g.2xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local SSDs or heavy floating-point computations. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, memory-intensive web serving, and general-purpose ARM64 development.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but lacks local storage and GPUs. The instance delivers strong memory performance, highlighted by a top-25% Passmark memory score and 774.63 GB/sec cached read bandwidth. However, raw CPU performance is relatively modest, with a Passmark CPU score in the bottom 25%. It lacks AVX and AMX extensions but supports AES cryptography. Network throughput is rated at a baseline of 4 Gbps. This instance is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis caching, database operations, and web serving, where high memory capacity and bandwidth are prioritized over raw compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 8 Graviton3 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton3 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores for caching and database workloads.

64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Strong memory performance with 774.63 GB/sec cached read bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimal for Redis caching, database operations, and web serving
